sections.paragraph:
ἑρμηνεύω (s. prec. two entries) aor. 3 sg. ἡρμήνευσεν (Papias [2:15]); pf. inf. ἑρμηνευκέναι Esth 10:31; pf. pass. ptc. ἡρμηνευμένην 2 Esdr 4:7 (Trag. et al.)

① to help someone understand a subject or matter by making it plain, explain, interpret (Pla., Ion, 535a al.; BGU 140, 20 [201/2 a.d.]; Philo) τινί τι someth. to someone Lk 24:27 v.l. Ἰουδαϊσμὸν ἑ. ὑμῖν propound the Judean way (Judaism) to you (w. the implication that interpretation has first been done) IPhld 6:1 (ἑρμ. also means simply proclaim, discourse on, without the idea of interpreting: Soph., Oed. C. 399; Philostrat., Vi. Soph. 2, 14; 2, 22 al.; Celsus 3, 55; SIG 1168, 88 [IV b.c.]; SEG VIII, 551, 39 [I b.c.]).

② to render words in a different language, translate (so the act. and mid., X., An. 5, 4, 4; BGU 326 II, 22; PRyl 62; 2 Esdr 4:7; Job 42:17b; TestSol 13:6 P.; EpArist 39; Philo; Jos., Ant. 6, 156, C. Ap. 2, 46) J 1:38 v.l., 42; 9:7 (Stephan. Byz. s.v. Ἱστός: κέλλα ῥαρσάθ, ὸ̔ ἑρμηνεύεται ἱστὸς νηός). Μελχισέδεκ is ἑρμηνευόμενος βασιλεὺς δικαιοσύνης Hb 7:2.—Of translation of Matthew’s work Papias (2:15).—DELG s.v. ἑρμηνεύς. M-M. EDNT. TW. Spicq.

prec. prec. = preceding

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

Papias Papias , early Christian writer, II a.d.—List 1

  1. pf. = perfect

inf. inf. = infinitive

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Celsus Celsus , in Origen, Contra Celsum, II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

act. act. = active

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Byz. Byz. = Byzantine, esp. in ref. to papyri

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

EDNT EDNT = Exegetical Dictionary of the NT—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 393
